narrative.debt_markdown:
- DECIDED: Adversarial-review MED #7 (gate.source as typed reference) DEFERRED to specs/contracts/step.md authorship. Why: requires naming writes.* slots; belongs in contract work, not Tier 0 scaffold. How to apply: when authoring step.md, replace opaque string source with discriminated union {kind: 'artifact' | 'checkpoint_response' | 'dispatch_result'; ref: string} + step-level superRefine validating the referenced write exists.
- DECIDED: Adversarial-review MED #11 (workflow spine_policy) DEFERRED to specs/contracts/phase.md. Why: requires workflow-vocabulary decisions that should be ratified alongside phase contract. How to apply: decide whether malformed workflows silently skipping canonical phases (Review, Verify) are rejected by schema or by contract test.
- DECIDED: Phase 1 contract stubs + behavioral tracks not yet authored: step.md, phase.md, run.md, selection.md, adapter.md, continuity.md, skill.md, session-hygiene.md, prose-yaml-parity.md, cross-model-challenger.md. Why: overnight scope was Tier 0 + Phase 0 closure + partial Phase 1 kickoff. How to apply: author in two batches per methodology — Discovery-lane first for domain refinements, Ratchet-Advance for final contracts. Each batch closes with a Codex adversarial property-auditor pass.
- DECIDED: Tier 2+ tooling deferred per ADR-0001 — no container isolation, no mutation testing, no property suites, no hidden-pool rotation, no anti-Goodhart ratchet machinery. Why: staged adoption Tier 0 → 3. How to apply: stay in Tier 0 until Phase 1 ratifies contracts; Tier 2 begins with first implementation slice.
- CONSTRAINT: Existing Circuit at ~/Code/circuit remains READ-ONLY reference. Why: it has users and serves as the Phase 0 evidence source. How to apply: if an insight requires modifying Circuit itself, defer to a post-circuit-next migration plan.
- CONSTRAINT: Codex handoffs via /codex skill (pipes to codex exec), NEVER codex:rescue subagent. Why: user preference saved at ~/.claude/projects/-Users-petepetrash-Code/memory/feedback_codex_handoff.md. How to apply: any Codex dispatch goes through /codex skill or the run-codex.sh wrapper; for Circuit workers use --adapter codex, which uses the same codex exec path.
- BLOCKED: Operator decision — whether to accept the type skeleton as-is or rerun Codex adversarial review after specs/evidence.md + specs/domain.md + specs/contracts/workflow.md landed. The prior Codex reviewer did not see those additions; a second pass might surface new objections or ratify the current state.
- BLOCKED: Operator decision — start Phase 1 with step.md (densest invariants; unlocks run.md + gate-source tightening) or run.md (most user-visible; unlocks event-log replay property tests).
narrative.goal: circuit-next Tier 0 is complete and validated. Phase 0 Evidence Loop closed with specs/evidence.md. Architecture-first type skeleton at src/schemas/ compiles under tsc --strict; 34 contract tests pass. Adversarial-review HIGH objections incorporated. Three commits on main, working tree clean. Ready for Phase 1 contract authorship.

Then DO: author first contract batch under Ratchet-Advance lane; close with Codex adversarial property-auditor pass per methodology.
